source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: Accomplishing important goals often requires sustained cognitive effort
Current results reveal systematic involvement of amygdala and NAcc throughout incentivized working memory task
Both regions represented incentive value and cognitive effort demands during every task period
Trial-to-trial associations between multivariate value coding and frontoparietal engagement and performance
Amygdala and NAcc activity correlated with frontoparietal activity suggesting coordinated operation
Findings extend prior work by providing insights into moment-to-moment contributions to sustaining effortful cognitive activity

typical_pattern: Opening statement of main finding or context
Elaboration of specific results with convergent evidence
Mechanistic interpretation linking findings to theory
Broader implications for understanding cognitive processes
Acknowledgment of limitations or alternative interpretations

likely_priorities: Mechanistic specificity of subcortical contributions to cognitive control
Temporal dynamics of value signals across task periods
Functional connectivity between subcortical and cortical regions
Generalizability to other cognitive tasks and populations
Distinction between state value and operation-specific value signals
likely_attacks: Task design limits direct comparison with prior studies on effort initiation
Unclear functional role of central amygdala activity
Generalizability to real-world tasks over longer timescales uncertain
Limited to monetary incentives and working memory in young adults
Alternative interpretation as state value signals not fully ruled out
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly acknowledges limitations including: task optimization limiting direct comparisons with prior studies, need for future work on CeA functional role, uncertainty about real-world task generalizability, and need to assess generalizability to other forms of motivation, executive functions, and participant populations. The paper also addresses alternative interpretations such as state value signals versus operation-specific signals, and discusses how findings relate to but differ from gating accounts.

examples.quoted_phrase: These regions may thus contribute to ongoing evaluations of whether it is worthwhile to continue exerting cognitive effort from moment to moment
The current results reveal the systematic involvement of the amygdala and NAcc activity throughout an incentivized working memory task
these findings significantly extend prior work by providing insights into the moment-to-moment contributions of BLA and NAcc representations to sustaining effortful cognitive activity
suggest that hot subcortical motivational processes may be even more of an inherent component of executive functions than previously recognized
the willingness to exert cognitive effort in a sustained manner may depend on value signals that are distributed across multiple brain regions

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, invented numbers, or author names were included. Inferences about reviewer preferences are grounded in the paper's explicit limitations section.
